test_cases.input: Script for a 22-minute tutorial on building a personal budget spreadsheet from scratch. 8 sections: intro, why budgets fail, setting up columns, entering income, categorizing expenses, the 50/30/20 rule, tracking for 30 days, outro. ~2,900 total words.
test_cases.output_summary: Generated 8 clean chapters starting at 0:00. Chapter names viewer-facing and specific ('Why Most Budgets Fail in Week One', 'Set Up Your Income Columns', etc.). All timestamps estimated with appropriate margin-of-error note. First timestamp correctly at 0:00. Format paste-ready for YouTube. Note suggested merging intro and 'why budgets fail' if under 2 minutes.

test_cases.input: Timestamped transcript from a 45-minute documentary-style interview on urban farming. Exact timestamps available. 12 natural topic shifts in the transcript.
test_cases.output_summary: Correctly identified exact timestamps from transcript (no estimation needed). Generated 12 chapters with viewer-facing names rather than generic 'Section 1' labels. Correctly noted this was from a timestamped transcript (no margin-of-error disclaimer needed). All chapters over 10 seconds. Format correct.

test_cases.input: Script for a 7-minute video on one specific photography technique (bokeh). Three sections: what bokeh is, how to achieve it, three common mistakes. ~900 words total.
test_cases.output_summary: Generated 4 chapters (including intro) with correct timestamps. Flagged that for a 7-minute video with only 3 content sections, chapters are sparse but still useful for navigation. Chapter names specific and viewer-facing. Correctly noted YouTube's 3-chapter minimum is met. One chapter name slightly generic ('Introduction') — minor issue; skill did not fully apply the 'avoid weak internal labels' criterion here.
